ksa_text: Ability to document and communicate architecture rationale, design decisions, and constraints to both technical and non-technical stakeholders with clarity and precision.

dcwf.top_element_label: Develop and document supply chain risks for critical system elements, as appropriate.

Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Data Architecture & Analytics is a primary or secondary domain for the VP Data Architecture. This ability KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (vp).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Data Architecture & Analytics), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Framework signal is weak (composite 0.437). External validation of this ability KSA for the VP Data Architecture role relies primarily on domain-level alignment rather than specific framework matches.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: DAMA-DMBOK, analytics maturity models (Gartner, Forrester), and analytics leadership surveys confirm data architecture and analytics competencies for senior roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: domain-level relevance of Data Architecture & Analytics for the VP Data Architecture is corroborated by DAMA-DMBOK, CDO survey data, and role-specific job market analysis, despite weak cross-framework signal.

decision_rationale: Data Architecture & Analytics ability KSA is within scope for the VP Data Architecture at the vp tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
